_metadata.title: Operational Reference Data for AI ITR App
_metadata.purpose: Runtime data feeds the engine needs in addition to the rules JSON
_metadata.disclaimer: All values must be verified against current CBDT notifications / Finance Act of relevant year before reliance.

surcharge_rates._note: Per Finance Act 2026 (assumed continuation of prior pattern). Verify against First Schedule.

capital_gains_cap._note: Surcharge on capital gains under these sections capped at 15% irrespective of income

dividend_cap._note: Surcharge on dividend income capped at 15% (similar treatment to capital gains)

marginal_relief._note: Surcharge limited so that (additional tax + surcharge) on additional income above threshold does not exceed the additional income itself

domestic_company_section_199_200_201._note: Companies opting for concessional rates pay flat 10% surcharge

cess.name: Health and Education Cess
cess.applies_to: ALL assessees including companies, firms, individuals, trusts

cost_inflation_index._note: Cost Inflation Index notified by CBDT under Sec 72(8). Used for indexed cost of acquisition / improvement.
cost_inflation_index._indexation_note_post_jul2024: FA 2024 amendment: indexation REMOVED for most assets transferred on/after 23 Jul 2024. Retained for resident individuals/HUFs on land/buildings acquired before that date (Sec 197(3) — option of 12.5% no-indexation OR 20% with-indexation, whichever lower).

compliance_calendar_2026_27._note: All dates assume FY 2026-27 / Tax Year 2026-27. Verify each year as dates shift.

return_due_dates.category: Company / auditable assessee / partner of audited firm

tax_audit_report_due.due: 30 September 2026 (1 month before return due date for audit assessees)

other_key_dates.form_10E_due: Before filing return for the year of arrears

decision_tree.question: Is the assessee a Company?
decision_tree.yes: ITR-6 (or ITR-7 if claiming Sec 11 / charitable)

decision_tree.question: Is the assessee a Charitable Trust / Political Party / RNPO?
Is the assessee a Firm / LLP / AOP / BOI?
Individual or HUF? Does the assessee have PGBP income?
Capital gains / foreign income / foreign assets / >1 HP / TI > ₹50 lakh / unlisted equity / director?
Resident, salary/pension/single HP/IFOS, agri ≤ ₹5,000, TI ≤ ₹50 lakh?
decision_tree.no: Default to ITR-2 (most flexible for individuals without PGBP)

itr_1_disqualifiers: Income from more than one house property
Foreign assets, foreign income, or signing authority abroad
Director in any company (verify under 2025 Act)
Held unlisted equity shares at any time during year
Has TDS u/s Sec 393 Sl.18 cash withdrawal (old 194N)
itr_4_disqualifiers: Held unlisted equity at any time
Business turnover > ₹2 crore (₹3 crore if cash receipts ≤ 5%)
Profession gross receipts > ₹50 lakh (₹75 lakh if cash ≤ 5%)

dtaa_top_10_summary._note: Simplified summary of withholding rates under top DTAAs. Always verify against the actual treaty text and any MLI modifications.
dtaa_top_10_summary._caveat: Royalty/FTS rates here are TREATY rates. Domestic Sec 9 rate (often 10% gross) may apply if more beneficial. Sec 159(4) gives benefit of more favourable.

treaties.capital_gains: Article 13 — most CG taxable in source State
treaties.notes: Limitation on Benefits (LOB) clause; needs TRC + Form 10F + 8233 if applicable
MLI signatory — covered by minimum standard PPT

treaties.capital_gains: Article 13 — alienation of property in India taxable in India
treaties.notes: Popular for Indian outbound investments. Treaty does NOT have specific FTS article.

treaties.capital_gains: Article 13 — partial exemption for shares acquired pre-1.4.2017 (grandfathering)
treaties.notes: Heavily amended via MLI; LOB-style limitation

treaties.capital_gains: Article 13 — partial source-state taxation post-2017 amendment for shares acquired post 1.4.2017
treaties.notes: Once popular for share investments — significantly diluted by 2016 protocol
MFN clause — rates may be lower if India agrees lower with another country

algorithm: 2. Compute gross income (same in both regimes)
3. Apply head-wise deductions (different — old allows HRA/LTC/etc; new doesn't)
4. Apply Chapter VIII (different — old allows all 80C-80U class; new only 124(2)/125/146)
5. Apply slabs (different — old standard slabs; new Sec 202 slabs)
7. Apply surcharge (new caps at 25%; old has 37%)
regime_comparison_engine.user_facing_explanation_template: Under OLD regime your tax is ₹{old_tax}. Under NEW regime your tax is ₹{new_tax}. {recommendation} regime saves you ₹{savings} for FY {year}. Reason: {explanation}.

switching_rules.individual_with_business_income: Can opt out only via Form before due date u/s 263(1); applies to subsequent years; ONE withdrawal lifetime; never again unless business income ceases
switching_rules.individual_without_business_income: Annual choice along with return — flexible

amounts.name: Actual HRA received
amounts.computation: HRA received during period of stay in rented accommodation

amounts.name: Rent − 10% of salary
amounts.computation: Annual rent paid MINUS 10% of (Basic + DA forming part)

hra_exemption_formula.salary_for_HRA: Basic + DA (forming part of retirement benefits) + commission as % of turnover
hra_exemption_formula.rent_proof_threshold: If rent > ₹1 lakh/year, landlord PAN MUST be provided (Form 12BB)

section_157_relief_algorithm.applicability: Salary/family pension arrears or advance pushing assessee into higher slab

steps.compute: Tax on TI INCLUDING arrears in current year
Tax on TI EXCLUDING arrears in current year
Incremental tax due to arrears: T1 - T2

steps.identify: Year(s) to which arrears actually relate

steps.compute: For each relating year: hypothetical incremental tax if arrear had been received then (using THAT year's slabs)
Sum of step 5 across all relating years

steps.result: Sec 157 relief = A - B (claim in current year's return)

tds_consolidated_lookup._note: Consolidated for Sec 393 (resident TDS). Verify thresholds annually.

salary_section_392.deduction_method: Average rate on estimated annual income

stamp_duty_value_sample._note: Stamp duty value (SDV) is the value adopted by State Govt for stamp duty. Updates by State revenue/registration department. Sample data only — integrate with state APIs where available.
stamp_duty_value_sample.ai_app_strategy: For MVP, ask user to enter SDV manually with link to state portal. For Phase-2, integrate top-3 state APIs.

equity_grandfathering_31jan2018._purpose: Sec 90(7) grandfathering for STT-paid LTCG equity acquired before 1 Feb 2018
equity_grandfathering_31jan2018._calculation: COA = MAX(actual cost, MIN(FMV on 31 Jan 2018, sale consideration))
equity_grandfathering_31jan2018._data_source: NSE/BSE archives — highest price quoted on 31 January 2018 (or most recent prior trading date)
equity_grandfathering_31jan2018._app_strategy: Build a lookup table with ISIN → 31jan2018 highest price for top 5000 equity scrips. For MFs, use 31 Jan 2018 NAV from AMFI archives. For unlisted, ask user.

common_section_80g_institutions._note: Sample list. Full list at incometaxindia.gov.in. App should build a search-enabled DB.
100_percent_no_limit: CM Relief Fund (each State — single fund)
Various notified funds — see Sec 133(1)(a) list
100_percent_with_qualifying_limit: Government / Local authority for promoting family planning (Sec 133(1)(a)(xxiii))
Indian Olympic Association / sports infrastructure (Sec 133(1)(a)(xxiv) — for companies)

app_cannot_do: Draft scrutiny responses or written submissions
Do transfer pricing documentation (Form 3CEB requires CA)
Certify accounts (Sec 63 tax audit — CA only)
Opine on M&A / business reorganisation tax effects
Represent users before tax authorities (only authorised reps under Sec 515)
Handle complex derivative / structured product tax effects
Respond to TDS/TCS notices on behalf of users
always_escalate_to_CA_when: User receives a scrutiny notice u/s 270
Cryptocurrency taxation > ₹10 lakh exposure (still emerging area)
Capital gains > ₹1 crore (review by CA recommended)

analysis.title: Section 234E TDS Late Fee Invalid for Pre-2015 Period Due to Lack of Legal Provision
analysis.summary:
The tribunal held that no late fee under Section 234E can be imposed for periods prior to 1 June 2015 due to absence of enabling provisions in Section 200A. The levy was deleted following consistent judicial precedents.

The post Section 234E TDS Late Fee Invalid for Pre-2015 Period Due to Lack of Legal Provision appeared first on TaxGu

analysis.title: ITAT Delhi Restores Appeal as Hyper-Technical View on Delay Defeated Justice
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal held that dismissal of appeal solely on limitation without considering bona fide reasons was unjustified. It restored the matter for adjudication on merits, emphasizing a liberal approach.

The post ITAT Delhi Restores Appeal as Hyper-Technical View on Delay Defeated Justice appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Assessment Set Aside Due to Need for Fresh Examination of Books & Fair Hearing: ITAT Delhi
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal set aside the assessment after noting that books of accounts were not produced and required reconsideration. It directed a fresh decision with proper opportunity, emphasizing procedural fairness.

The post Assessment Set Aside Due to Need for Fresh Examination of Books & Fair Hearing: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru.<

analysis.title: Section 194T: TDS Rules for Partnership Firms and LLP Partners
analysis.summary:
The Finance Act, 2024 has inserted a new section, 194T, for TDS deduction by partnership firms/Limited Liability Partnerships (LLPs). Section 194T takes effect from 1st April, 2025 (AY 2026-27), to bring payments such as salary, remuneration, commission, bonus and interest (whether on capital or on loan) by a partnership firm/LLP to its partners under the […]

The post Section 194T:

analysis.title: Safe Harbour Rules under New Income Tax Act, 2025 & Rules, 2026- Part II
analysis.summary:
The framework outlines mandatory disclosures and timelines for opting into safe harbour. Key takeaway: strict compliance is essential to gain tax certainty.

The post Safe Harbour Rules under New Income Tax Act, 2025 & Rules, 2026- Part II appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Form 122 : One Declaration, Complete Salary TDS Control
analysis.summary:
The new form consolidates multiple income disclosures into one, enabling accurate TDS deduction. Key takeaway: unified reporting reduces excess tax deduction and refunds.

The post Form 122 : One Declaration, Complete Salary TDS Control appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Bombay HC Quashes Section 270A Penalty Due to Absence of Under-Reported Income
analysis.summary:
Court held that penalty under Section 270A cannot apply where assessed income does not exceed processed income. Key takeaway: statutory conditions must be strictly met.

The post Bombay HC Quashes Section 270A Penalty Due to Absence of Under-Reported Income appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: No Section 69A Cash Deposit Addition Without Proper Inquiry & Evidence: ITAT Mumbai
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal held that the assessee had adequately explained the source of cash deposits with supporting evidence. Addition under Section 69A was deleted as the AO failed to rebut the explanation.

The post No Section 69A Cash Deposit Addition Without Proper Inquiry & Evidence: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Penny Stock Allegation Rejected Due to Lack of Evidence Against Share Transactions
analysis.summary:
The tribunal ruled that reliance only on an investigation report without independent evidence cannot justify treating LTCG as bogus. Additions under Section 68 and commission were deleted.

The post Penny Stock Allegation Rejected Due to Lack of Evidence Against Share Transactions appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: ITAT Rejects TP Adjustment Due to Incorrect Benchmarking of Power Tariff
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal held that industrial tariff charged by electricity boards is the correct benchmark for CPP transactions. It rejected the use of generator procurement rates adopted by the TPO.

The post ITAT Rejects TP Adjustment Due to Incorrect Benchmarking of Power Tariff appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Reassessment Notice Time-Barred as Issued Beyond Limitation Period: Karnataka HC
analysis.summary:
The Court held that reassessment proceedings must be initiated within the statutory time limit. It found the notice issued after the deadline to be invalid.

The post Reassessment Notice Time-Barred as Issued Beyond Limitation Period: Karnataka HC appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Complete TDS Rate Chart for FY 2026-27 with Section-Wise Breakdown
analysis.summary:
A detailed overview of updated TDS provisions mapping old and new sections, rates, and thresholds. Helps taxpayers understand compliance requirements across all payment categories.

The post Complete TDS Rate Chart for FY 2026-27 with Section-Wise Breakdown appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Loose Sheets and Digital Data Not Enough: ITAT Quashes Additions for Lack of Evidence
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal held that additions cannot stand without a clear link between seized material and the assessee. It ruled that third-party documents without corroboration lack evidentiary value.

The post Loose Sheets and Digital Data Not Enough: ITAT Quashes Additions for Lack of Evidence appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Human Rights Commissions Ignore Lynching of Muslims: Hon’ble Mr Justice Atul Sreedharan
analysis.summary:
The court examined whether NHRC can direct investigations unrelated to human rights violations. It held that such actions may exceed statutory limits under existing law.

The post Human Rights Commissions Ignore Lynching of Muslims: Hon’ble Mr Justice Atul Sreedharan appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Channel owners cannot be taken as comparables for distributors for transfer pricing purpose
analysis.summary:
ITAT Delhi held that companies owning and operating channels cannot be compared with distribution company for the purpose of transfer pricing. Accordingly, directs TPO to exclude such comparables for benchmarking distribution segment and determination of ALP.

The post Channel owners cannot be taken as comparables for distributors for transfer pricing purpose appeared first

analysis.title: Gold Monetisation Scheme 2026: Earn Interest on Idle Gold at Home
analysis.summary:
The scheme allows households to deposit gold and earn interest without selling it. The key takeaway is that both interest and capital gains are fully tax-exempt.

The post Gold Monetisation Scheme 2026: Earn Interest on Idle Gold at Home appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: ITAT Deletes PF/ESI Disallowance as Section 143(1) Adjustment Held Beyond Scope
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal examined disallowance made for delayed employee contributions under Section 143(1). It held that debatable issues cannot be adjusted at the processing stage, resulting in relief to the assessee. The ruling clarifies procedural boundaries.

The post ITAT Deletes PF/ESI Disallowance as Section 143(1) Adjustment Held Beyond Scope appeared first on

analysis.title: ITAT Quashes Section 271(1)(c Penalty as Unsigned SCN Held Void in Law
analysis.summary:
The issue involved validity of penalty proceedings initiated through an unsigned notice. The Tribunal ruled that such a notice is invalid and cannot confer jurisdiction. The decision highlights the mandatory requirement of proper authentication.

The post ITAT Quashes Section 271(1)(c Penalty as Unsigned SCN Held Void in Law appeared first on TaxGur

analysis.title: Section 11 Exemption Allowed as Property in Trustees’ Names Held Not Beneficial to Them
analysis.summary:
The case examined whether property registered in trustees’ names violated Section 13(1)(c). The Tribunal held no violation as no benefit accrued to trustees, allowing exemption under Section 11.

The post Section 11 Exemption Allowed as Property in Trustees’ Names Held Not Beneficial to Them appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: TDS Default Not Applicable as Section 197 Certificate Covers Entire Assessment Year
analysis.summary:
The case examined whether a lower TDS certificate applies prospectively or for the full year. The Court held it applies to the entire assessment year, negating default and interest liability.

The post TDS Default Not Applicable as Section 197 Certificate Covers Entire Assessment Year appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Pass-Through Costs Excluded From Transfer Pricing PLI as No Value Addition Was Performed: ITAT Mumbai
analysis.summary:
ITAT Mumbai held that payments made to third-party vendors on a cost-to-cost basis, without any value addition by the assessee, should be treated as pass-through costs and excluded from PLI computation.

The post Pass-Through Costs Excluded From Transfer Pricing PLI as No Value Addition Was Performed: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on

analysis.title: Section 269ST Penalty Deleted as Seized Excel Sheets Did Not Prove Single Cash Transaction Above ₹2 Lakh
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal held that consolidated Excel entries showing aggregate cash sales were insufficient to establish receipt of ₹2 lakh or more in a single transaction, and penalty under Section 271DA could not be sustained.

The post Section 269ST Penalty Deleted as Seized Excel Sheets Did Not Prove Single Cash Transaction Above ₹2 Lakh appeared firs

analysis.title: Bogus Purchase Addition Deleted Because Material Movement, Site Records & Banking Proof Established Genuineness: ITAT
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal deleted purchase disallowances after noting invoices, transport records, weighbridge slips, tax returns and bank payments supported actual procurement and use of materials in infrastructure projects.

The post Bogus Purchase Addition Deleted Because Material Movement, Site Records & Banking Proof Established Genuineness:

analysis.title: Share Transfer in Family Arrangement Not Taxable: ITAT Deletes ₹489 Crore Addition
analysis.summary:
The issue was whether a share transfer without consideration constituted taxable capital gains. The Tribunal held that genuine family realignment is not taxable.

The post Share Transfer in Family Arrangement Not Taxable: ITAT Deletes ₹489 Crore Addition appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: ITAT Rejects Appeal Where Declared Capital Gains Accepted Without Adjustment
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal held that since the Assessing Officer made no addition after verifying disclosures, the grievance lacked merit. Grounds were rightly treated as infructuous due to absence of tax impact.

The post ITAT Rejects Appeal Where Declared Capital Gains Accepted Without Adjustment appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Mandatory PAN Requirement Introduced for Post Office Transactions Under Income Tax Rules 2026
analysis.summary:
The consolidation into Form 121 introduces stricter documentation and reporting obligations. The decision emphasizes accountability and structured tax reporting mechanisms.

The post Mandatory PAN Requirement Introduced for Post Office Transactions Under Income Tax Rules 2026 appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: As a Foreign Company, Do I Need to File Annual CIT in China?
analysis.summary:
The issue is whether foreign businesses without offices in China must comply with tax laws. The key takeaway is that activities and income sources can trigger CIT obligations even without physical presence.

The post As a Foreign Company, Do I Need to File Annual CIT in China? appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Reopening Invalid as Notice Issued Beyond Six-Year Limitation Period: SC
analysis.summary:
The issue was whether reassessment notice issued after six years was valid. The Court upheld that such notices are time-barred and cannot be sustained under law.

The post Reopening Invalid as Notice Issued Beyond Six-Year Limitation Period: SC appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Delhi HC Quashed Reopening Notice as Issued Beyond Six-Year Limitation
analysis.summary:
The Court examined whether reassessment notice issued beyond limitation was valid. It held that notices issued after expiry of the six-year limit under the old regime are barred and liable to be set aside.

The post Delhi HC Quashed Reopening Notice as Issued Beyond Six-Year Limitation appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Reopening Invalid Without Independent Material Showing Escapement: Gujarat HC
analysis.summary:
The Court held that reassessment under Sections 147/148 cannot be initiated solely on third-party data without independent evidence of income escapement. It ruled that such reopening amounts to suspicion and lacks the required “reason to believe.”

The post Reopening Invalid Without Independent Material Showing Escapement: Gujarat HC appeared first on

analysis.title: Section 80GGC Deduction Denied as Donation Linked to Bogus Political Party Activities
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal upheld disallowance of deduction where donations were routed back to donors through layered transactions. The key takeaway is that non-genuine donations do not qualify for tax deduction.

The post Section 80GGC Deduction Denied as Donation Linked to Bogus Political Party Activities appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Philosophy of Income Tax Act 2025: Perspectives from Great Thinkers
analysis.summary:
The new tax law addresses long-standing concerns over complexity by reducing provisions and simplifying language. It introduces structural clarity and digital tools to improve compliance while reducing taxpayer burden.

The post Philosophy of Income Tax Act 2025: Perspectives from Great Thinkers appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Cloud Service Payments Not Taxable as Royalty Due to No Transfer of IP Rights: SC
analysis.summary:
The Supreme Court affirmed that payments for cloud computing services are not royalty where no intellectual property rights are transferred. It held that mere access to services does not amount to use of equipment or technology under tax law.

The post Cloud Service Payments Not Taxable as Royalty Due to No Transfer of IP Rights: SC appeared first on

analysis.title: Date of DRP Upload, Not Visibility in AO Module, Determines Receipt: Telangana HC
analysis.summary:
The Court held that the issue of treating DRP upload as receipt was already settled by earlier rulings. It dismissed the Revenue’s appeals while making the decision subject to the Supreme Court’s final outcome.

The post Date of DRP Upload, Not Visibility in AO Module, Determines Receipt: Telangana HC appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: TP Segment Split Rejected as Trading & After-Sales Services Are Inextricably Linked; TNMM at Entity Level Upheld: ITAT Delhi
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal held that trading and service activities were inextricably linked and could not be segmented. It accepted entity-level TNMM, rendering TP adjustments unsustainable.

The post TP Segment Split Rejected as Trading & After-Sales Services Are Inextricably Linked; TNMM at Entity Level Upheld: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru

analysis.title: TDS Credit Can’t Be Denied Merely Because It Appears in Sister Concern’s 26AS: ITAT Delhi
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal held that TDS credit cannot be denied merely because it does not appear under the assessee’s PAN. It ruled that the assessee cannot be forced to ensure revision of TDS returns by the deductor.

The post TDS Credit Can’t Be Denied Merely Because It Appears in Sister Concern’s 26AS: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Only Legitimate Tax Can Be Collected; ITAT Restores BBC Distribution Fee Royalty Taxability Issue
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal set aside the dismissal of a delayed appeal, holding that the issue of distribution fee taxability requires fresh examination on merits. The case was remanded to the Assessing Officer for reconsideration with proper hearing.

The post Only Legitimate Tax Can Be Collected; ITAT Restores BBC Distribution Fee Royalty Taxability Issue appeared fi

analysis.title: Refund of Earlier Advance Can’t Be Taxed as Accommodation Entry U/s 68: ITAT Delhi
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal held that return of advances cannot be taxed under Section 68. The key takeaway is that explained transactions supported by records cannot be treated as unexplained income.

The post Refund of Earlier Advance Can’t Be Taxed as Accommodation Entry U/s 68: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: No Penalty Where Return Filed After Probate Grant and Income Fully Disclosed in Form 26AS
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal held that delay in filing the return due to pending probate proceedings was beyond the control of executors. It ruled that such delay constituted a bona fide explanation, leading to deletion of penalty under Section 270A.

The post No Penalty Where Return Filed After Probate Grant and Income Fully Disclosed in Form 26AS appeared first on

analysis.title: No TDS on Foreign Agent Commission for Services Rendered Abroad; ITAT Deletes Section 40(a)(i) Disallowance
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal held that commission paid to foreign agents for services rendered outside India is not taxable in India. Consequently, no TDS obligation arises, and disallowance under section 40(a)(i) was deleted.

The post No TDS on Foreign Agent Commission for Services Rendered Abroad; ITAT Deletes Section 40(a)(i) Disallowance appeared first on

analysis.title: No Section 43B Disallowance as Service Tax Not Claimed in P&L: ITAT Dehradun
analysis.summary:
The tribunal recalled its earlier order after finding it addressed an incorrect issue. It ultimately upheld that no disallowance applies when no expense is claimed.

The post No Section 43B Disallowance as Service Tax Not Claimed in P&L: ITAT Dehradun appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: No Adhoc Disallowance of Commission on Assumptions Without Specific Defects: ITAT Delhi
analysis.summary:
The issue was whether commission payments were genuine business expenses. The Tribunal held that disallowance based on non-response and suspicion was not justified. The key takeaway is that conjectures cannot replace evidence in tax assessments.

The post No Adhoc Disallowance of Commission on Assumptions Without Specific Defects: ITAT Delhi appeared first on

analysis.title: Foreign Tax Credit Cannot Be Denied for Partial Overseas Return Filing: ITAT Mumbai
analysis.summary:
The Tribunal held that FTC claims cannot be rejected solely due to incomplete foreign tax return filing. The key takeaway is that authorities must verify available and subsequent evidence before denying relief.

The post Foreign Tax Credit Cannot Be Denied for Partial Overseas Return Filing: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Search on Third Party Cannot Extend Reassessment Time Without Assessee Link: SC
analysis.summary:
The Supreme Court held that reassessment notices issued after the six-year limit are barred. It affirmed that expired cases cannot be revived under later provisions.

The post Search on Third Party Cannot Extend Reassessment Time Without Assessee Link: SC appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Captive Power Valuation Must Include Electricity Duty for 80-IA Deduction: Calcutta HC
analysis.summary:
The issue concerned overlap of deductions under Sections 80-IA and 80HHC. The Court held that 80HHC profits cannot be reduced by 80-IA deduction, ensuring independent computation.

The post Captive Power Valuation Must Include Electricity Duty for 80-IA Deduction: Calcutta HC appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Cash Deposits from Tractor Sales Can’t Be Taxed as Unexplained Income: ITAT Ahmedabad
analysis.summary:
The tribunal held that cash deposits backed by sales records cannot be treated as unexplained income. It upheld deletion of addition where transactions were properly documented.

The post Cash Deposits from Tractor Sales Can’t Be Taxed as Unexplained Income: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Bogus Purchase Addition Remanded as Fresh Evidence Warrants Verification: ITAT Surat
analysis.summary:
The assessee produced new evidence including invoices and tax returns of suppliers. The Tribunal held that these documents must be examined before deciding the issue.

The post Bogus Purchase Addition Remanded as Fresh Evidence Warrants Verification: ITAT Surat appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: No Capital Gains Tax on Void Sale Deed Executed Through Forged POA: ITAT Ahmedabad
analysis.summary:
The issue involved taxation of capital gains based on a disputed land sale. The Tribunal held that once the sale deed was declared void, no capital gains could arise.

The post No Capital Gains Tax on Void Sale Deed Executed Through Forged POA: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Commission Agent Not Eligible for TDS Credit on Farmers’ Income: ITAT Ahmedabad
analysis.summary:
The tribunal held that TDS credit cannot be claimed by an agent when the underlying income belongs to farmers. It ruled that credit must align with the person offering the income to tax.

The post Commission Agent Not Eligible for TDS Credit on Farmers’ Income: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: SC Dismisses Revenue Appeal as Assessment Based on Guesswork Without Proof
analysis.summary:
The issue was whether addition based on estimated yield was valid. The SC upheld that without material evidence, such additions are unsustainable and cannot be based on suspicion.

The post SC Dismisses Revenue Appeal as Assessment Based on Guesswork Without Proof appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Delay Condoned with Cost: ITAT Grants Fresh Chance, Slams Non-Complianc
analysis.summary:
The issue involved dismissal of appeal due to delay and non-appearance. The tribunal condoned the delay citing medical reasons and remanded the matter for fresh assessment, imposing cost for non-compliance.

The post Delay Condoned with Cost: ITAT Grants Fresh Chance, Slams Non-Complianc appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: U/s 153C Valid but Addition Fails: No Incriminating Material = No Deemed Dividend
analysis.summary:
The issue was addition of deemed dividend under search assessment. The tribunal held that without incriminating material, additions in completed assessments are unsustainable.

The post U/s 153C Valid but Addition Fails: No Incriminating Material = No Deemed Dividend appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Reassessment After 4 Years Quashed as No Failure to Disclose Material Facts: Bombay HC
analysis.summary:
The issue was whether reassessment could be initiated after four years without fresh evidence. The court held such reopening invalid when based on existing records and no failure of disclosure.

The post Reassessment After 4 Years Quashed as No Failure to Disclose Material Facts: Bombay HC appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: 870-Day Delay Not Condoned: ITAT Refuses Relief, Calls Out Negligence & “No Sufficient Cause”
analysis.summary:
The issue was whether a long delay in filing appeals could be condoned. The tribunal held that absence of credible explanation bars condonation, leading to dismissal of appeals.

The post 870-Day Delay Not Condoned: ITAT Refuses Relief, Calls Out Negligence & “No Sufficient Cause” appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Wrong Section Claim Not Fatal: ITAT Remands Matter & Nullifies Penalty
analysis.summary:
The issue was denial of capital gains exemption due to claim under wrong section. The tribunal held that a genuine claim cannot be rejected merely for citing an incorrect provision and remanded the case.

The post Wrong Section Claim Not Fatal: ITAT Remands Matter & Nullifies Penalty appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Penalty U/s 270A Quashed: No Specific Charge of “Misreporting” = No Penalty
analysis.summary:
The issue was whether penalty for misreporting can be imposed without specifying the applicable clause. The tribunal held such penalty invalid and ordered its deletion.

The post Penalty U/s 270A Quashed: No Specific Charge of “Misreporting” = No Penalty appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Gauhati HC on a humane approach & powers of Commissioner under Section 264
analysis.summary:
The Court noted that the taxpayer could not upload large volumes of documents due to technical limits. It held that authorities should adopt a practical and humane approach, including on-site verification. Failure to do so vitiates proceedings.

The post Gauhati HC on a humane approach & powers of Commissioner under Section 264 appeared first on T

analysis.title: TDS & TCS Changes from 1st April 2026
analysis.summary:
The reform consolidates TDS provisions into a structured system and introduces digital compliance mechanisms. It enhances clarity, reduces errors, and improves reporting efficiency.

The post TDS & TCS Changes from 1st April 2026 appeared first on TaxGuru.

analysis.title: Reassessment Quashed for Wrong Sanction: ITAT Bangalore Strikes Down 147 Order for Breach of Section 151
analysis.summary:
The issue was whether reassessment notice issued without approval from the correct authority is valid. The tribunal held it invalid and quashed the assessment for lack of jurisdiction.

The post Reassessment Quashed for Wrong Sanction: ITAT Bangalore Strikes Down 147 Order for Breach of Section 151 appeared first on TaxGuru
